author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Command name is /beads-design. Clean, descriptive, follows beads-* naming convention.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Architecture is delegation -- orchestrator calls existing /beads-brainstorm, /beads-plan, /beads-deepen, /beads-plan-review in sequence. Zero code duplication. Updates to individual commands automatically propagate.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Smart pauses -- interactive brainstorm phase, auto-run plan, pause to confirm plan before deepen, then auto-chain deepen + plan-review.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Phase gates with recovery -- each phase runs its existing verification. On failure, pause and offer retry/skip/abort.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Auto-apply safe feedback from plan-review. Only pause for trade-off decisions requiring user judgment.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Default to Comprehensive detail level (user can override). This command is for the full thorough pipeline.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: No skip flags. Auto-detect existing brainstorm context from bead ID. Keep the interface simple.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Follows /lfg precedent for compound commands that chain multiple steps.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Parallelism is preserved from existing commands -- each already maximally parallel internally (deepen spawns 20-40+ agents, plan-review runs 4 agents concurrently). Model hints (haiku/sonnet/opus) inherited.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Output style is progress banners at phase transitions + clear verification status per phase + final consolidated summary with epic bead ID, child bead count, decisions captured, and review findings.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: npx smoke tests need conditional bd install step (same pattern as test-installation job) — bd not in CI by default

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: execFileSync with array args prevents shell injection entirely — use instead of execSync with template strings for any user-controlled input

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Command counts must be consistent across README, CATALOG, plugin.json description, pre-release-check.sh, and CI yaml — update all 5 locations when adding commands

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Extracted args.find() result to _positional variable to avoid double evaluation; paths starting with '-' remain unsupported (acceptable edge case for CLI usage)

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added exitCode parameter to usage() defaulting to 1; --help callers pass 0 explicitly — cleaner than duplicating print logic

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Math.max(0, rows - 3) prevents negative contentHeight; also replaced magic +20 scroll buffer with Math.max(contentHeight * 4, 80) so long descriptions scale with terminal size

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Always quote $GITHUB_WORKSPACE in shell steps — self-hosted runners may have spaces in workspace paths

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added --provenance and --access public to npm publish; added id-token: write permission for SLSA provenance attestation

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Already resolved in Wave 1 via _positional extraction in bdcompound-807.9 fix — single args.find() call

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Set timeout: 30000 (30s) to match plan-view.js pattern but longer since export is a one-shot operation not interactive

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Already resolved in Wave 1 — replaced contentHeight+20 with Math.max(contentHeight*4, 80) so buffer scales with terminal size

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Pinned to bun 1.3.6 (current installed version) and setup-bun@v2; this is a build-only tool so minor version drift risk is low but pinning is good hygiene

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed process.on('exit', cleanup) — all exit paths (SIGINT, SIGTERM, 'q' key) already call cleanup() explicitly before process.exit(); the handler was a redundant double-call

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Removing process.on('exit', cleanup) from a raw-mode TUI creates a terminal corruption class: any die() or unexpected exit path bypasses cleanup. Always keep an 'exit' event handler as the catch-all, not just SIGINT.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: continue-on-error: true on a conditionally-guarded CI step creates a double-silence: beads unavailable = skip, beads available but failing = also skip. Only use continue-on-error when you truly want silent failure; conditional guards are the right way to handle optional external dependencies.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: When command counts change, update CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/plugin-catalog.md, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, README.md, plugin.json, pre-release-check.sh, and CI YAML (8 locations total). CLAUDE.md and plugin-catalog.md were missed this cycle.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Restored process.on('exit', cleanup) as defensive practice. Investigation showed die() calls all happen before setRawMode(true) in current code (buildTree runs at line 474, setRawMode at 487), so no current terminal corruption. However the handler is a zero-cost safety net for future code changes that might call die() after raw mode is active. Prior removal was based on correct analysis but removed a valuable defensive layer.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed continue-on-error: true — the conditional guard handles beads unavailability; continue-on-error on top was silencing real failures. Conditional guard = skip when unavailable. No continue-on-error = fail when available but broken.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Raised bdExecFile timeout from 10000ms to 30000ms to match plan-export.js. The failure mode in plan-view (silent empty string) is worse than plan-export (explicit error), so if anything plan-view needs the longer timeout more. Both tools call the same bd binary on the same data.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Updated OpenCode/Gemini thresholds in pre-release-check.sh from 25 to 29 to match CI verify-release job. Both files have SYNC: comments — they must always match. The Cortex threshold was left at 25 because CI does not check Cortex outputs in verify-release.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Updated command counts to 29 in CLAUDE.md (2 places), plugin-catalog.md (header + 3 missing entries added), and marketplace.json description. Also added beads-design, beads-quick, beads-import to plugin-catalog.md table.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used workflow_run trigger instead of needs: — cross-workflow dependencies require workflow_run in GitHub Actions. npm-publish now only fires when Test Installation workflow completes successfully AND the triggering event was a release.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced curl|bash with brew install beads on macos-latest runners. Homebrew is a package manager with version pinning, checksums, and signing — eliminates the supply chain risk entirely. Also eliminates the fragile availability check since brew install fails clearly if the package is unavailable. macos-latest runners ship with Homebrew pre-installed so no extra setup needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Moved validateBeadId to entry point only (after beadId is parsed, before any fetching begins). Removed redundant per-call-site guards — execFileSync already prevents injection, and the ID doesn't change between calls. Also fixed: requires grouped at top of plan-export.js, process.stderr.write -> console.error in plan-view.js.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added Cortex command count check (>= 29) to verify-release job to match pre-release-check.sh. Added --frozen-lockfile to bun install in verify-release for reproducibility. Both changes sync the CI gate with the local pre-release script.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Plan deepened with research from 7 agents (4 research, 3 review). Key improvements: (1) Config moved from .beads/memory/ to .beads/config/ due to merge=union corruption risk, (2) Bead .5 rescoped from filesystem scanning to config-based injection due to security/complexity concerns, (3) Review Context must use XML wrapping to prevent prompt injection, (4) Cross-check validation scoped to warnings-only with high-value checks

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Store config in .beads/config/project-setup.md not .beads/memory/. Reason: .beads/memory/ uses merge=union gitattributes which would corrupt YAML frontmatter during git merges. .beads/config/ provides clean separation (memory=knowledge, config=settings).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Dropped filesystem scanning of ~/.claude/agents/ and .claude/agents/ for dynamic agent discovery. Reasons: (1) CRITICAL security risk - arbitrary .md files injected into prompts, (2) ~/.claude/agents/ writable by any process including malicious npm postinstall, (3) YAGNI - no current user complaint. Rescoped to: read project-setup config and inject Review Context into subagent prompts.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: yaml-parser.ts line 15 has a bug: yamlLoad.CORE_SCHEMA is undefined (yamlLoad is a function, not an object). This causes silent fallback to DEFAULT_SCHEMA which allows !!js/function tags. Fix: import CORE_SCHEMA directly from js-yaml. This is a pre-existing bug, not part of this epic, but should be fixed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Plan review (second pass) found 1 P1 critical (yaml-parser CORE_SCHEMA bug), 8 important suggestions, 4 minor. Key structural changes: merge .5 into .2, drop .6 or extract to orchestrator, simplify .1 (no profiles/monorepo/version), simplify .3 Sources (freeform list). Protected artifacts need 6 locations updated, not 1.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Applied plan review feedback. 14 recommendations: 11 applied across 6 beads, 2 structural (close .5, close .6), 1 new bead (.9 for component counts). Key changes: merged .5 into .2, dropped .6, simplified .1 (no profiles/monorepo/version), simplified .3 Sources (freeform list), added agent allowlist to .2, added PR field sanitization to .7, added dedup note to .8.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Sources section added as freeform bullet list in epic description template with explicit Brainstorm: entry type; child beads get a References subsection (subset of epic sources relevant to that child). Cross-check step 5.5 placed between bead creation and final review as warning-only with AskUserQuestion gate — does not block submission.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: project-setup SKILL.md uses disable-model-invocation:true and XML critical_sequence tags (matching beads-knowledge pattern) so the agent follows a strict ordered flow: check existing -> detect stack -> select agents -> optional context -> write config. This matches the existing skill conventions.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: .beads/config/ provisioning belongs in the memory setup step ([3/9]) of install-claude.sh, not as a separate step. Both memory and config are .beads/ subdirs provisioned at install time, so grouping them keeps the step count clean and the logic cohesive.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Agent uses a four-step ORM-agnostic algorithm (detect ORM → list migrations → list schema artifacts → cross-reference) rather than ORM-specific top-level logic, so future ORM adapters can be added without changing the core flow.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Drizzle's out directory is configurable via drizzle.config.ts — agent must parse the config file to find the actual migration path rather than hardcoding 'drizzle/', otherwise detection silently misses all migration files.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used anchor-tag reference (#brainstorm-detected) within the decision tree to avoid repeating the 'what to do when found' block — keeps the three detection paths (label, recent keyword, old keyword) DRY while each path clearly jumps to the same handler

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Label-based detection must check both the input bead AND its parent — when planning a phase from a brainstorm epic, the input bead is a child (phase), not the brainstorm itself; the brainstorm label lives on the parent epic

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: migration-drift-detector added as agent 15 alongside data-migration-expert (13) and deployment-verification-agent (14) in the conditional agents section. The two migration agents have a clear division: data-migration-expert owns migration CODE correctness, migration-drift-detector owns schema/migration CONSISTENCY. Trigger conditions expanded from Rails-only to all 5 ORM patterns (Rails, Alembic, Prisma, Drizzle, Knex) plus schema artifact paths. Dedup note added to Step 2 of Finding Synthesis to warn that both agents flag migration files and findings should be deduplicated.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: beads-parallel injects review_context into all 3 agent prompt templates (standard, ralph, teams) under '## Project Conventions' — empty string when config missing, so no behavioral change for projects without project-setup.md

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: beads-review.md already had migration-drift-detector wired as a conditional agent (not in original bead scope) — allowlist was updated to reflect all 15 actual review agents, not the 14 documented in memory

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Component counts updated to 29 agents (15 review) and 16 skills after landing migration-drift-detector agent and project-setup skill. Six files updated: plugin.json, marketplace.json, plugin-catalog.md, pre-release-check.sh, CLAUDE.md, installers/install-claude.sh.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Fontsource packages for Geist are @fontsource/geist-sans and @fontsource/geist-mono (not @fontsource/geist). Original plan had wrong package names.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Research conflict resolved -- stagger pattern. Research showed data-reveal-delay attributes cannot be used in CSS calc() cross-browser. Updated to inline --index custom property pattern: style='--index: N' + calc(var(--index) * 80ms).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Research conflict resolved -- Tailwind integration. Use @tailwindcss/vite as Vite plugin, NOT @astrojs/tailwind (deprecated for TW4). Import syntax is @import 'tailwindcss' not @tailwind directives.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Plan revised after research. Changes: (1) font package names corrected, (2) stagger pattern changed from data attributes to --index custom property, (3) easing updated to (0.22,1,0.36,1), (4) accessibility: no-motion-first + noscript fallback added, (5) copy button UX enhanced with 1500ms checkmark + aria-label states + localStorage persistence, (6) tab default changed to npx (most recognized), (7) SEO requirements added (JSON-LD, OG image), (8) counter animation clarified as @property + JS play-state hybrid.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Plan reviewed and locked. 9 child beads. Review findings: architecture(0H/4M/6L), simplicity(2H/1M/2L), security(0H/3M/4L), performance(2H/1M/3L). Auto-applied: 14 safe fixes. User-decided: cut counter animation, cut localStorage, move to local image assets, defer deployment bead. All beads updated with research+review findings.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: @fontsource/geist-sans registers as font-family 'Geist Sans' (with space). @fontsource/geist-mono registers as 'Geist Mono'. Per-weight CSS files available at 100-900.css.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Astro 6 font preload paths use /_astro/ prefix at build time. Actual filenames depend on Vite's content hashing -- the preload hints may need adjustment after first build if filenames don't match.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Astro components with slots work cleanly for terminal chrome wrappers -- the slot receives raw HTML content and the component handles all chrome styling. Using arbitrary Tailwind values like bg-[#ef4444] for the traffic light dots avoids needing to extend the theme config.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Astro site uses Tailwind v4 with @theme directive for custom tokens (bg, surface, border, text, accent). The data-reveal system in Base.astro uses a shared IntersectionObserver -- components just add data-reveal attribute to opt in. No per-component JS needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Pipeline component uses Tailwind v4 @theme tokens (bg-bg, text-accent, border-border, etc.) and the global data-reveal IntersectionObserver with --index CSS custom property for stagger delays. No separate observer needed per component.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Astro inline scripts in InstallCta use typed Record for tab-to-command mapping with in-memory state only (no localStorage). Copy button uses innerHTML swap for icon changes with setTimeout restore. Tab aria-selected and class toggling handled manually via classList add/remove.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Hero section uses NO data-reveal attribute to ensure immediate LCP paint. Terminal mockup is hidden on mobile via hidden lg:block on the perspective-transform wrapper, keeping mobile payload light while desktop gets the tilted 3D terminal effect.

author: Roberto Mello
text:
LEARNED: TerminalMockup uses a plain with no wrapper -- terminal content lines should use

tags with color classes rather than 

 blocks to stay consistent with the component's leading-relaxed styling

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: command-map iframe height needs calc(100dvh - 64px) to account for the 64px Nav bar. Using 100dvh without subtraction causes content to overflow below the viewport.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Astro 6 + Tailwind 4 static build produces 496KB total output including self-hosted Geist fonts (all weights). CSS bundle is 20KB. Build time ~720ms. Very lean.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Font preload hrefs in Base.astro use hardcoded paths like /_astro/geist-sans-latin-400-normal.woff2 but Vite content-hashes the filenames (e.g., geist-mono-latin-400-normal.LC9RFr9I.woff2). The preload hints may not match -- needs a build-time solution or Astro font API.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: model_profile wired as prose instructions in command .md files (not code), since commands are markdown processed by Claude agents. Critical agents annotated with '-- add model: opus if profile=quality' inline at each Task() dispatch site.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Critical agents are security-sentinel, architecture-strategist, goal-verifier, performance-oracle. Affected commands: lavra-review (3 agents), lavra-eng-review (3 agents), lavra-work single-bead (goal-verifier), lavra-work multi-bead Phase M8 (goal-verifier), lavra-ship (goal-verifier). lavra-design calls lavra-eng-review as a subcommand so it inherits automatically.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced 150-line bead description cap with completeness gate (all required sections present, zero judgment calls for the agent) and ~1000 LOC scope budget per bead. Rationale: agents drift when specs are vague, not when specs are long. Inspired by Mario Zechner's observations on agent-driven coding.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added --no-parallel flag to /lavra-work (non-default, opt-in). When set, overrides max_parallel_agents to 1 and pauses for user review between each bead. Rationale: the bottleneck is review quality, not code generation speed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added prompt injection defense to knowledge system matching config file treatment -- sanitize on read (strip role prefixes, bidirectional chars), wrap in untrusted-knowledge XML tags, grep -iF for search. Shell injection was already blocked by jq escaping and CSV import.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Catalog drift checks belong in PR CI (test-compatibility job), not just release gates. Release-only checks are reactive — drift merges silently then blocks tagging. For a published marketplace, both ghost entries (in catalog, no file) AND missing entries (file exists, not in catalog) should be hard failures.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: When grepping CATALOG.md for command names in pre-release checks, constrain to table rows only (lines starting with |). Prose descriptions reference command names inline (e.g., 'Included in /lavra-design') and produce false positives if the whole document is searched.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Astro 6 glob loader normalizes collection entry IDs: lowercased + dots stripped (not just .md extension). Examples: AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION.md → id: autonomous_execution. releases/v0.7.0.md → id: releases/v070 (dots stripped from the whole id path, not just extension). Section slug arrays in Doc.astro and index.astro must use lowercase IDs with underscores matching the loader output — hyphens and uppercase will not match. This is an Astro 6-specific behavior, undocumented, and different from Astro 5 (which only stripped .md). Discovered when sidebar showed empty Configuration and Releases sections after upgrade to Astro 6.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Config/security boundary doctrine for Lavra. (1) Storage separation: .beads/config/ for settings, .beads/memory/ for knowledge — never mix them. merge=union in memory/ is for conflict-free JSONL merging; applying it to YAML/JSON config files corrupts frontmatter. (2) Agent context injection: always XML-wrap injected content with <untrusted-knowledge> tag + strip role prefixes (SYSTEM:/ASSISTANT:/[INST]) + strip Unicode bidirectional override chars (U+202A-U+202E, U+2066-U+2069) + strip null bytes before injection into system messages. (3) Safe search: use grep -iF (fixed-string, case-insensitive) not grep -i — prevents regex metacharacter injection when search terms come from bead titles or other user-controlled content. Known limitation: bidi-strip sed uses codepoint escapes that are not portable across all sed implementations — best-effort, not guaranteed. Newline-in-JSON gap: SANITIZED_KNOWLEDGE content is not newline-escaped before JSON embedding (unlike ESCAPED_STATE), so multi-line entries are best-effort safety only. Applies to: auto-recall.sh, any new hook reading .beads/ content into agent context.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Use PATTERN: type when capturing something meant to be reused as a convention — not just recording a one-time decision. The clearest signal: can you write it as a rule? 'When doing A, do B' = PATTERN. 'We chose A over B in this case because of C' = DECISION. LEARNED vs PATTERN overlap: LEARNED is for one-time discoveries that surprised you, not rules you would apply again. If you are filing a naming convention or architectural constraint as LEARNED, it is probably a PATTERN. Borderline example from this codebase: 'use beads- prefix for all workflow commands' is a PATTERN (a rule to follow every time). 'Renamed /deepen-plan to /beads-deepen for consistency' is a DECISION (a specific application of that pattern). One PATTERN entry replaces the need for many scattered DECISION entries that all reference the same underlying convention. This entry is itself a meta-example: it describes when to use PATTERN, and is filed as PATTERN so it is recalled when classifying future knowledge entries.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Conventional commit type for infrastructure and config-only changes: use chore: for DNS records (CNAME), CI config changes, gitignore/gitattributes, dependency bumps, and non-user-facing scripts/tools. Use ci: for CI-specific changes (either is acceptable, ci: is more discoverable). These are the cases most likely to slip through as untyped commits. Untyped commits break changelog generation and git log --grep filtering. Example: 'add CNAME for lavra.dev custom domain' should be 'chore: add CNAME for lavra.dev custom domain'.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Both ghost commands (in catalog, no file) and missing commands (file exists, not in catalog) are hard failures. Warn-vs-fail asymmetry was reviewed and rejected — for a published marketplace, undocumented commands are a defect, not a warning. Both directions exit 1.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: CATALOG.md command extraction must be constrained to table rows (lines starting with |) using grep -E '^\|' before extracting command names. CATALOG.md descriptions reference other commands inline (e.g., 'Included in /lavra-design') which produce false positives if the full document is searched.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Catalog accuracy check added to test-compatibility CI job (runs on every PR to main) rather than only verify-release (release-only). Drift that silently merges into main then blocks release tagging is prevented by catching it at PR time.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Inventory complete. Lavra writes to 3 .beads/ subdirs: .beads/memory/ (knowledge.jsonl, recall.sh, knowledge-db.sh, session-state.md, .lavra-version, SQLite cache), .beads/config/ (lavra.json, project-setup.md, codebase-profile.md), .beads/retros/ (date.json snapshots). Hooks (auto-recall.sh, memory-capture.sh, provision-memory.sh) have hardcoded .beads/ paths throughout. Commands reference .beads/memory/ and .beads/config/ in their markdown bodies. Uninstaller does NOT clean up .beads/ data by design (preserves knowledge).

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: beads backup/ dir (.beads/backup/) is owned by the beads CLI, not Lavra. Migration must not touch it.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Folder-level .lavra/.gitignore and .lavra/.gitattributes chosen over subdirectory-level. Cleaner — one file controls all ignore/merge rules for the directory. Previously .beads/memory/ had its own .gitattributes and .gitignore which was fragmented.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Hard recode .beads/ → .lavra/ (no dual-path fallback, no LAVRA_DIR env var). Migration script handles existing installs. Simpler hooks = fewer bugs.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Auto-migrate on install: copy + warn. Copy .beads/{memory,config,retros} to .lavra/. Leave .beads/ originals. Print warning: 'Old Lavra data preserved in .beads/ — safe to delete after verification'. No interactive prompt, no auto-delete.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Scope mode: HOLD — plan is right-sized. 6 phases, mechanical path changes with clear verification. No scope expansion or reduction needed. Highest risk: Phase 4 (commands have .beads/memory/ in prose text, not just shell commands — grep verification is the right acceptance test).

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: .gitattributes and .gitignore move to .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/). Paths inside them use memory/ prefix: 'memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union', 'memory/knowledge.db'. This is the key structural difference from the old setup.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: .lavra-version marker moves to .lavra/.lavra-version (root of .lavra/), not .lavra/memory/.lavra-version. auto-recall.sh must be updated to match in Phase 2.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: subagent-wrapup.sh and knowledge-db.sh need ZERO changes. knowledge-db.sh is fully path-agnostic (all paths passed as parameters). subagent-wrapup.sh has no .beads/ refs.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: .lavra-version is at .lavra/.lavra-version (root), not .lavra/memory/. auto-recall.sh needs a LAVRA_DIR variable separate from MEMORY_DIR to read the version file correctly.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Use cp -rn (no-clobber recursive) for migration to avoid overwriting if .lavra/ already exists. Migration must be idempotent.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: .beads/backup/ in lavra-ship.md must NOT be changed — it is owned by the beads CLI, not Lavra. All other .beads/ refs in commands should be updated.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: releases/v0.7.0.md and other historical release notes must NOT be updated. They document what was true at that version. Only update current/active docs.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: 772 total .beads/ references found across ~50 files. After all phases, final grep check should find zero non-exception results. Exceptions: .beads/backup/ in lavra-ship.md, migration detection code in installers, releases/v*.md historical notes.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Plan deepened with research from 7 agents (architecture, security, simplicity, deployment, data-migration, goal-verifier, performance). Key improvements: (1) Platform copies (opencode/, gemini/, cortex/) added to Phase 4 scope — 82+ files missed in original plan. (2) Migration function hardened: TARGET validation, SQLite exclusion, stale .gitattributes/.gitignore exclusion, post-copy line count check, idempotent re-run protection. (3) Migration smoke test (upgrade scenario) added to Phase 6. (4) check-memory.sh fallback hardcode at line 97 identified as easily-missed critical fix in Phase 2.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: cp -rn destination behavior: if destination dir does NOT exist, copies contents. If it DOES exist, copies into it. mkdir -p for .lavra/retros/ MUST precede cp -rn for retros migration. This is a known cp quirk that causes wrong-path bugs.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Old .beads/memory/.gitattributes and .beads/memory/.gitignore MUST be excluded from migration copy. If they land in .lavra/memory/, they create redundant subdirectory-level attribute/ignore files that conflict with the new .lavra/ root-level files. Exclude them explicitly in the migration loop.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: git check-attr merge .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl is the correct post-install verification for merge=union. Must be in Phase 6 smoke test checklist.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Version 0.8.0 (minor bump). Structural directory change with migration is significant enough for minor version. Not a patch.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Plan review found: (1) migrate_beads_to_lavra() must be called BEFORE provision_memory_dir() — reversed order causes idempotency guard to skip migration on empty knowledge.jsonl; (2) check-memory.sh line 38 and auto-recall.sh lines 32-38 both guard on .beads/ existence, must check .lavra/ for non-beads projects; (3) Phase 4 should re-run conversion scripts (bun run convert-opencode.ts) not manually sed platform copies — prevents drift

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Scenario 2 (migration) is missing a negative idempotency test: run installer a second time on a project that already has .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl with content — migration must be SKIPPED without overwriting existing data. Add this as Scenario 2b: run install.sh again on /tmp/test-lavra-migrate and verify .lavra/memory/knowledge.jsonl is unchanged (same line count as after first migration).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Applied plan review feedback. 14 recommendations applied across 5 beads (bdcompound-63c.2, .1, .3, .4, .6). Key changes: (1) Phase 3 call order inverted — migrate_beads_to_lavra() before provision_memory_dir() in all installers; (2) Phase 2 scope expanded to fix check-memory.sh line 38 guard and auto-recall.sh lines 32-38 guard (both guard on .beads/ existence, silent failure for non-beads projects); (3) Phase 4 changed to re-run conversion scripts instead of manual sed on platform copies; (4) Security: auto-recall.sh bare heredoc JSON replaced with jq assembly, session-state.md gets sanitization pipeline; (5) Migration function simplified — removed path traversal re-validation, config exclusion logic, and post-copy line count check

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Moved all .lavra/ management to folder-level .gitignore and .gitattributes at .lavra/ root (not .lavra/memory/). Paths inside use memory/ prefix: 'memory/knowledge.jsonl merge=union', 'memory/knowledge.db'. .lavra-version moves to .lavra/.lavra-version. mkdir uses -m 700 for all subdirs. git add drops -f flag to respect user .gitignore.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: auto-recall.sh guard changed from 'if .beads/ missing exit' to 'if both .beads/ AND .lavra/ missing exit'. Non-beads projects with .lavra/ can now use lavra. LAVRA_DIR separate var used for version file path at .lavra/.lavra-version vs MEMORY_DIR for .lavra/memory/. All bare heredoc JSON replaced with jq -cn assembly (H1 fix). session-state.md now sanitized and wrapped in untrusted-knowledge (M1 fix). CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation uses realpath canonical check AFTER early-exit guards (H3 fix + perf).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added migrate_beads_to_lavra() to shared-functions.sh (shared by all 4 platform installers). Migration called BEFORE provision_memory_dir — order is load-bearing: provision creates empty knowledge.jsonl which would satisfy -s idempotency guard. Idempotency uses -s (non-empty check) not -f. Symlinks skipped with -L check. SQLite .db files and old gitconfig files excluded from memory/ copy.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: beads-*/lavra-compound/deepen/parallel commands in opencode/gemini/cortex platform dirs are stale files not regenerated by conversion scripts (no canonical sources). Had to apply sed directly after conversion script re-run. Conversion scripts only copy files that exist in plugins/lavra/commands/ — stale platform-only files persist until manually removed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: .gitignore and .gitattributes move to .lavra/ ROOT — not .lavra/memory/. smoke tests in github-release.md and test scripts checking these files must use .lavra/.gitignore (not .lavra/memory/.gitignore). paths inside the files use memory/ prefix.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Bump to 0.8.0 (minor version) — structural directory change from .beads/ to .lavra/ warrants minor bump per semantic versioning. All 5 smoke test scenarios pass: fresh install, migration, migration idempotency, .lavra/ gitignore warning, OpenCode and Gemini platform installers. pre-release-check.sh passes 19/19.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Changed bd show --json to --long in single-bead Phase 1. --long includes comments in readable format; --json requires jq parsing and subagents were not reading comments at all.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: testing_scope branches at plan-time not implementation-time. The spec locks in test coverage targets before any code is written — changing the spec template is the correct intervention point. The implementing agent follows the spec.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Goal verification runs per-wave at orchestrator level, not inside subagent prompts. Subagents do self-review; orchestrator verifies the spec was actually met. Separation of concerns: subagent knows the code, orchestrator knows the bead spec.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used bd show --long and shell text parsing (awk/sed) rather than --json + jq. --long output is stable and human-readable; jq parsing of nested JSON was causing orchestrator mistakes. The script is the reliability boundary.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Orchestrator pre-processes bead context via extract-bead-context.sh before building subagent prompts. Subagents receive clean markdown, not raw JSON. The script is the reliability boundary — orchestrator mistakes from jq parsing are eliminated.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Installer config walkthrough belongs in the skill (project-setup), not the installer shell script. Installer nudges toward the skill; skill owns the interactive config flow. Separating the two avoids duplicating config logic across global and per-project install paths.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Global vs per-project install intent is ambiguous when user runs installer from inside a project directory. Detecting .git/.beads in CWD and asking before proceeding prevents silent global installs that confuse users.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: parse_installer_args() shared function with LAVRA_HOOKS_ARE_GLOBAL flag. Cortex hooks are always global so the project-detection question is suppressed for that platform. Other platforms (claude, opencode, gemini) support per-project hooks so the question is shown.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: extract-bead-context.sh uses an inline sanitize_untrusted_content() fallback so it stays self-contained if the shared library is not installed. Fallback must be kept in sync with sanitize-content.sh manually.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Script resolves library path relative to its own location: installed path (.claude/hooks/) tried first, source tree path (plugins/lavra/hooks/) tried second. This lets the script work in both dev and installed contexts without configuration.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: testing_scope branches at plan-time (lavra-design Phase 6, lavra-plan Testing template). By the time lavra-work runs, the spec already specifies coverage targets. Changing the implementing agent's behavior is too late — the spec is the intervention point.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: goal-verifier runs per-bead before commits in Phase M8. CRITICAL failures (Exists/Substantive) reopen the bead via bd update --status open and exclude it from the wave commit. WARNINGs proceed but are noted in the wave summary.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Orchestrator pre-processes all bead context before dispatching subagents. extract-bead-context.sh is the reliability boundary — subagents receive clean, sanitized markdown, never raw JSON requiring inline jq. Agent mistakes from jq parsing are eliminated at the source.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: bd show --long includes comments in human-readable format in a single call. Subagents were reading descriptions but not comments, missing all INVESTIGATION/FACT/PATTERN findings from research agents. Switching from --json to --long closes this gap without any jq.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: BSD sed silently ignores \x{NNNN} unicode escapes — bidi strip in sanitize-content.sh is a complete no-op on macOS; use tr -d with ANSI-C $'\uNNNN' quoting for portable unicode character deletion

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: TITLE extracted from bd show output was not sanitized before injection into untrusted-knowledge block — all user-contributed fields (title, description, findings) must pass through sanitize_untrusted_content before prompt injection

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: extract-bead-context.sh in scripts/ is never copied by any installer — scripts that need to run in target projects must be in plugins/lavra/hooks/ and listed in the hook copy loop of each platform installer

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: set -euo pipefail + tr -d empty input can return exit 1 on BSD — sanitize_untrusted_content() pipeline must end with || true to prevent silent script death in command substitutions

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Phase M6 inline sanitization spec in lavra-work.md diverged from the shared sanitize-content.sh library immediately after extraction — command files that describe sanitization steps must reference the shared library rather than duplicating the spec

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: when adding a new hook/script that targets installed projects, always verify it appears in the hook copy loop of all three platform installers (install-claude.sh, install-gemini.sh, install-opencode.sh)

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: goal-verifier in multi-bead wave path — 'orchestrator already has bead details in memory' claim is ambiguous for bd ready input path; bd show must be called explicitly in Phase M1 for all paths before the validation section is referenced

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: $'\uNNNN' ANSI-C quoting is not supported in bash 3.2 (macOS default) — produces literal string not Unicode codepoint; use printf '\xNN...' for portable UTF-8 byte sequences in tr -d arguments

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: grep -E (ERE) does not support Perl-style lookaheads like (?!...). Both macOS BSD grep and GNU grep reject them. Use two-stage grep (match then exclude) instead.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: ((var++)) returns exit code 1 under set -e when var=0 because bash arithmetic treats the pre-increment value 0 as falsy. var=$((var + 1)) always exits 0. pre-release-check.sh already guarded with || true so it was exempt.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: sourced sanitize-content.sh early (SCRIPT_DIR_EARLY) to avoid naming conflict with the later SCRIPT_DIR used by the sqlite block. Sanitization runs inline in the CONTENT extraction pipeline via | sanitize_untrusted_content | head -c 2048.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: ((var++)) under set -e was missed in scripts/select-opencode-models.sh (starts at i=1 so does not crash immediately, but the pattern is wrong). Always grep the full repo after applying a set-e arithmetic fix, not just the reported files.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: source of a shell library before early-exit guards adds overhead on the hot path of a PostToolUse hook. The script's own comment explained this pattern but the new source was added above the guards, violating it. Move library sources to after the primary grep guards.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: memory-capture.sh pipeline subshell in import-plan.sh -- PREV_BEAD mutations inside a piped while loop never propagate to the parent shell. Use process substitution (done < <(...)) instead of a pipeline when the loop must mutate caller variables.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: knowledge-db.sh kb_search() uses an unquoted heredoc (<<SQL not <<'SQL') which allows shell expansion of $FTS_QUERY into the SQL statement. The term extractor is the only injection guard. Should use a quoted heredoc or bound parameters.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: select-opencode-models.sh had ((i++)) at line 101 inside a while loop under set -euo pipefail; replaced with i=$((i + 1)) to avoid exit-on-zero when i=0. This was the one file missed in the earlier PR #6 / bdcompound-grw fix pass.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Fixed SQL injection in kb_search() by replacing unquoted heredoc (<<SQL) with sqlite3 bound parameter syntax -- FTS_QUERY is now passed as a positional ? arg after the SQL string, so it is never shell-expanded or interpolated into the query. TOP_N stays interpolated because it is validated as numeric (^[0-9]+$) earlier in the function, making it safe.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: sqlite3 CLI accepts bound parameters as positional arguments after the SQL string: sqlite3 db.sqlite 'SELECT ... WHERE col MATCH ?' "$VALUE". This eliminates shell expansion of the variable into the SQL text. For LIMIT, a pre-validated numeric variable is safe to interpolate directly because there are no SQL-special characters in digits.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Moved source sanitize-content.sh to after both grep guards in memory-capture.sh and consolidated SCRIPT_DIR_EARLY + SCRIPT_DIR into a single SCRIPT_DIR defined once post-guards. This avoids sourcing the file on every PostToolUse:Bash invocation (~3-5ms overhead) and only runs on the ~1% of calls that pass all guards.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: sanitize_untrusted_content() is only called inside the prefix loop at the CONTENT extraction line (line 66), so sourcing sanitize-content.sh immediately after the grep guards — before the CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation and MATCH_LINE extraction — guarantees the function is defined before it's needed with no functional behavior change.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Bash pipeline stages run in subshells -- variables mutated inside a piped while loop (cmd | while) are discarded when the loop exits. The PREV_BEAD variable in import-plan.sh was never propagating back, so every child bead after the first was created without --deps.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Fixed by converting 'awk ... | grep ... | while' to process substitution form 'while ... done < <(awk ... | grep ...)'. This keeps the while loop body in the main shell so PREV_BEAD assignments persist across iterations. Process substitution is compatible with set -euo pipefail.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: select-opencode-models.sh starts i=1 so ((i++)) does not crash on the first iteration, masking the bug. Still wrong — the pattern is inconsistent and will break if i is ever reset to 0.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used sqlite3 bound-parameter syntax (positional ? argument) rather than switching to quoted heredoc. Quoted heredoc would prevent ALL variable expansion including TOP_N, requiring additional changes. Bound parameters are cleaner and more idiomatic.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: sqlite3 CLI supports parameterized queries: sqlite3 db.sqlite 'SELECT ... WHERE col MATCH ?' "$VALUE". The value is passed as a positional argument after the SQL string, not shell-expanded into it. TOP_N is safe to interpolate directly since it is validated as ^[0-9]+$ earlier.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Placed SCRIPT_DIR definition immediately after the grep guards (before CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR validation). This is slightly earlier than strictly necessary (sanitize_untrusted_content is only called in the prefix loop), but matches the natural boundary of the early-exit section and avoids interleaving setup code with logic.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: In bash, all stages of a pipeline run in subshells. PREV_BEAD mutations inside a piped while loop are lost on exit. Process substitution (done < <(cmd)) keeps the while loop in the main shell while still piping input from a subshell command.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used tr -cd 'A-Za-z0-9._-' after the sed extraction to make sanitization intent explicit at the use-site. The sed regex already constrains the character set, so tr is redundant in practice but serves as visible documentation of the invariant. A second empty-check guards against tr stripping the entire value.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Combined sed passes 2 and 3 into a single alternation: 's/...(2>&1|\|\||&&|\||;).*$|["'\''][[:space:]]*$//' — also added explicit || before | per bdcompound-7wa. Both beads touch the same expression so combined into one atomic change.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: || is handled correctly by the old single-| branch (| matches first pipe, .* eats rest), but adding it explicitly makes the intent self-documenting. Merged with o5q sed consolidation since both touch the same line.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Chose Option A (inline) over B/C/D. File indirection via skill files has a runtime failure mode on every non-project-install path (global Claude, Gemini, OpenCode, source repo). LLMs handle 1000-line prompts fine -- command size was never the root cause of review-skipping.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Review language unified: /lavra-review ALWAYS runs. review_scope=full runs on all changes; review_scope=targeted runs only on P0/P1 or arch/security beads. Removed all 'self-review only' framing that implied self-review was a complete substitute for /lavra-review.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: test-features.sh Test 9 was failing because it checked lavra-work.md for Deviation Rules, goal-verifier, session-state, commit_granularity, and Locked/Discretion/Deferred -- all of which were in single-bead.md. Inlining fixed all 5 failures.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Cursor sessionStart hook output format confirmed. Uses additional_context field (string) in stdout JSON, NOT hookSpecificOutput.systemMessage like Claude Code. Real-world example: printf '{"additional_context":"..."}'. This means knowledge injection IS possible via sessionStart.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Cursor hooks.json lives at .cursor/hooks.json (project) or ~/.cursor/hooks.json (user). Format: {version:1, hooks:{eventName:[{command:..., type:command, timeout:N}]}}. Event names are camelCase (sessionStart, afterShellExecution, subagentStop). No TeammateIdle equivalent exists in Cursor.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Cursor hook event mapping to Claude Code equivalents: sessionStart->sessionStart (same), afterShellExecution->PostToolUse:Bash, subagentStop->SubagentStop, stop->Stop. Cursor also has preToolUse, postToolUse, beforeShellExecution, subagentStart, afterFileEdit, beforeSubmitPrompt, preCompact, afterAgentResponse. NO TeammateIdle equivalent.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Cursor stdin JSON for sessionStart includes: session_id, is_background_agent, composer_mode + common fields: conversation_id, generation_id, model, hook_event_name, cursor_version, workspace_roots, user_email, transcript_path. Notably: cwd is NOT present (Claude Code sends cwd). Need workspace_roots[0] as equivalent.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Cursor hooks auto-recall must use workspace_roots[0] instead of .cwd for project dir detection. auto-recall.sh reads cwd from stdin: CWD=. For Cursor this must become: CWD=. This is the key adaptation needed in the hook.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: No conversion script needed for Cursor. Cursor uses same .md format as Claude Code for commands and agents — but commands/agents are NOT installed (Cursor does not support slash commands or custom agent types). Only hooks and memory system are installed. This keeps install-cursor.sh simple: hooks + memory, nothing else.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Cursor installer follows same structure as install-gemini.sh: banner, ownership check, hooks copy, memory provision, hooks.json write. Skip conversion step (no bun/typescript needed). ~150 LOC.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Cursor hooks.json version:1 format with camelCase events. Project hooks: .cursor/hooks.json. User hooks: ~/.cursor/hooks.json. Environment vars provided to scripts: CURSOR_PROJECT_DIR, CURSOR_VERSION, CURSOR_USER_EMAIL, CURSOR_TRANSCRIPT_PATH.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Cursor subagents docs confirm full custom agent support. Format: markdown with YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, readonly, is_background). File locations: .cursor/agents/ (project), ~/.cursor/agents/ (user), AND .claude/agents/ (Claude compatibility — Cursor reads these natively). This means Lavra agents work in Cursor without conversion if installed to .claude/agents/.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Cursor plugin system has .cursor-plugin/plugin.json manifest. Supports: rules, skills, agents, commands, MCP servers, hooks. Components discovered automatically from default directories. Plugin installed to ~/.cursor/plugins/local/ for local testing. Marketplace at cursor.com/marketplace.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Cursor is a full port target (not memory-only). Agents work via .claude/agents/ compatibility. Commands need investigation (.cursor/ or .claude/ format?). install-cursor.sh scope expands to: hooks + memory + agents (via .claude/ compat) + optional plugin bundle. Update bead description to reflect this.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: OpenCode agents docs confirm full custom agent support (opencode.ai/docs/agents). Two definition methods: (1) opencode.json config under 'agent' key, (2) markdown files with YAML frontmatter in ~/.config/opencode/agents/ (global) or .opencode/agents/ (project). Fields: description, mode (primary/subagent/all), model, prompt, tools, permission, steps, temperature, hidden, color. The knowledge base entry 'OpenCode agents cannot be ported, has 4 predefined types only' is WRONG — that was stale/incorrect. OpenCode agents are nearly identical format to Cursor subagents.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: bd prime (beads global SessionStart hook) instructs agents to use 'bd remember' for persistent knowledge. This bypasses knowledge.jsonl entirely and won't be surfaced by auto-recall.sh. Fixed by adding explicit override in auto-recall.sh system message output.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Cursor natively reads .claude/agents/ for Claude compatibility — Lavra agents work in Cursor without any conversion. Precedence when names conflict: .cursor/ > .claude/ > .codex/

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Cursor subagents are markdown files with YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, readonly, is_background). Invoked via /name or @name syntax or automatic delegation based on description field.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: OpenCode fully supports custom agents — the prior knowledge entry 'has 4 predefined types only' was wrong (verified opencode.ai/docs/agents Apr 2026). Markdown files in .opencode/agents/ with YAML frontmatter: description, mode (primary/subagent/all), model, prompt, tools, permission.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Cursor hooks.json uses camelCase events (sessionStart, afterShellExecution, subagentStop). sessionStart outputs additional_context field (confirmed via securityreviewai/securityreview-kit-cursor real-world example). Stdin uses workspace_roots[0] not cwd for project dir.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: bd prime's 'bd remember' instruction competes with Lavra's knowledge.jsonl convention. Fixed in auto-recall.sh: convention note now appears in both first-run orientation and standard recall output, overriding bd prime before any command is invoked.
